entries.definition:
z-m-h-r (possibly an extension of root ز/م/ه z-m-h) to be
angry, to be red in the face and eyes with rage; to glitter; to be
gleeful; to be freezing cold. Of this root, only زَمْهَرِير zamharīr
occurs once in the Qurʾan.

زَمْهَرِير zamharīr [n.] extreme coldness (76:13) لَا يَرَوْنَ فِيهَا شَمْسًا
وَلَا زَمْهَرِيرًا seeing there neither sun [scorching heat] nor biting
cold.

404
زَنْجَبِيل

زَنْجَبِيل zanjabīl [n., a loan word, from either Persian or Syriac,
occurring once in the Qurʾan] ginger (76:17) وَيُسْقَوْنَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا كَانَ
مِزَاجُهَا زَنْجَبِيلًا and they will be given a drink infused with ginger.
